dissent_logged: Technical seat's disciplined scale-zone plan (341-360 confluence, CONFIRM >365 held 2+ sessions, stop <341, 4:1 R:R) argues the setup itself is sound and the position is trivial (0.15% of book) so the cost of a small scale-in is low. This is overruled by the compounding weight of Sentiment (CROWDED, no capitulation) + Smart-money (DISTRIBUTING, HIGH confidence, insiders selling into the exact zone the plan calls 'buy') + the fact that both the cheap-PEG story and the moat story lean on the same unverified $100B FY27 guidance figure. Logged as dissent, not acted on: if the desk wants optionality on the disciplined technical setup, a name-only price alert at the CONFIRM level (>365 held 2 sessions) costs nothing and preserves the trade idea without capital committed now.
cio_memo: No add. The standing '350-360' plan was a bare price-zone trigger with no capitulation or insider-behavior filter attached — exactly the kind of naive 200d buy the Skeptic warns about. Price is in the zone, but the zone is being sold into by insiders (HIGH-confidence distribution, $620M sold vs $1.9M bought TTM, June cluster including the founder), sentiment has not flushed despite a 27.5% drawdown (consensus static, short interest only 1.32%), and momentum is broken below the 50d — the deterministic scorecard already forecloses BUY. Fundamental quality is real (PEG 0.42, 49% op margin, 37% ROE) but that valuation and the 'hard to displace' moat both rest on the same unverified $100B FY27 backlog figure, which is guidance/visibility, not signed contract — and Google's TPUv9 defection to MediaTek is proof the moat is not absolute. NVDA's Nov-2021→Oct-2022 path (dominant franchise, blowoff top, insider selling, consensus intact through the first leg, then -66%) is a live analog, not a scare story. Given the desk's own late-cycle AI-bubble-defense stance and preference for RSP/AVUV over single-name AI adds, this is not the moment to average into a stub position on a name showing this exact combination of red flags. Drop the standing plan as written; keep the 4-share stub untouched (immaterial size, no forced sell); re-open only on hard, falsifiable evidence per the alert conditions below.
circle_of_competence: Broadcom earns money selling custom AI/networking ASICs co-designed with hyperscalers (Google TPU, others) plus core networking/RF/broadband chips, and from VMware enterprise infrastructure software acquired in 2023. It is hard to displace because hyperscaler custom-silicon programs take years of joint engineering to re-platform and VMware's enterprise virtualization stack carries very high switching costs — but neither moat is absolute, as Google's TPUv9 shifting toward MediaTek shows.
skeptic_response: Accept. The unflushed-200d-plus-insider-distribution argument is the strongest case against adding and there is no evidence to rebut it: no offsetting insider buy cluster, no capitulation signature (short interest stuck at 1.32%, consensus unchanged through a 27.5% drawdown), and the smart-money seat's HIGH-confidence read is a direct, dated observation (June insider sales including Samueli's ~$720M proposed sale, plus CFO/CLO), not a modeled inference. A genuine '200d bounce' setup normally coincides with sentiment washout and insiders stepping up, not insiders stepping out into the same support level retail is being told to buy. Accepting this argument is also why the standing plan — a bare price trigger with no capitulation or insider-behavior filter — gets cancelled rather than merely 'watched'.
standing_add_plan_update: CANCEL the 'add toward 8-10sh at 350-360' plan as originally specified — do not add at current price (358.31) or anywhere in the 341-360 zone on price alone. Keep the existing 4-share stub (~$1,433, 0.15% of book) unchanged; it is too small to matter either way. Only re-underwrite an add if BOTH a fundamental/behavioral condition and the technical CONFIRM trigger are met (see alert_conditions) — a price-only trigger is explicitly disqualified by this review.
alert_conditions: Form 4 shows an insider OPEN-MARKET BUY cluster (not option exercise/RSU vest) within the 341-360 zone -> re-open the add case immediately
Daily close > $365 held for 2+ consecutive sessions (technical CONFIRM) AND no insider distribution in the prior 4 weeks -> eligible for a scaled add with stop at weekly close < 341, target 408.85
Weekly close < $341 -> setup fully invalidated, drop AVGO from the watchlist, no re-entry discussion until a new base forms

Next earnings print: AI/backlog guide raised above the $17.2B street number AND management quantifies backlog as signed/contracted (not 'visibility') -> resolves the unverified-$100B concern, upgrade narrative
A second hyperscaler defection beyond Google->MediaTek TPUv9 within 2 quarters -> confirms moat crack, hard avoid regardless of price
Meta or Amazon publicly recommits to Broadcom custom silicon -> bullish confirmation, offsets the Google defection
Short interest or a sentiment survey shows a capitulation spike (short interest meaningfully above 1.32%, consensus estimates cut) -> the 200d-test pattern becomes credible again
